text.title: SEO Audit Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every SEO Audit Runbook application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Phase 1 (Crawl) MUST produce: full URL inventory, status-code distribution, orphan-URL list, duplicate-title/description list. Missing any artefact = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without a current crawl, every subsequent phase analyses stale data.

rule.statement: Phase 2 (On-Page) MUST sample at least the top-100-traffic URLs by GSC clicks and audit each against on-page-seo-checklist-2026. Findings filed with URL + checklist-item-id + severity. <Top-100 = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Top-traffic URLs concentrate revenue; on-page issues there have outsize fix-impact.

rule.statement: Phase 3 (Content Decay) MUST compare GSC clicks/impressions audit-window vs prior equivalent window, flag URLs with ≥30% decline still ranking top-20, assign each to {refresh, merge, delete, redirect}.
rule.rationale: Decay is the highest ROI for established sites and the easiest to skip.

rule.statement: Phase 4 (Links) MUST audit: internal-link graph for orphan/under-linked top-of-funnel pages, external backlink growth/loss, toxic-backlink candidates (if any). All three sub-audits required.
rule.rationale: Internal-only misses link-loss attacks; external-only misses internal cluster gaps.

rule.statement: Phase 5 (Tech-SEO) MUST verify: Core Web Vitals (field data, not lab), indexability (robots.txt, X-Robots-Tag, canonical chains), structured-data validation, hreflang for multilingual, mobile parity. Lab CWV = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Lab numbers mislead on real-user performance and lose engineering credibility.

rule.statement: Phase 6 (Report) MUST emit executive summary (≤1 page), findings backlog ranked by ICE or RICE, recommended sequencing (quick wins ≤30 days, structural ≤60), sign-off block. Unranked backlog = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Unranked backlogs rot in Notion; ranking turns the audit into work.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
